2603.25158.title: Trace2Skill: Distill Trajectory-Local Lessons into Transferable Agent Skills
2603.25158.paper_summary: Equipping Large Language Model (LLM) agents with domain-specific skills is critical for tackling complex tasks. Yet, manual authoring creates a severe scalability bottleneck. Conversely, automated skill generation often yields fragile or fragmented results because it either relies on shallow parametric knowledge or sequentially overfits to non-generalizable trajectory-local lessons. To overcome this, we introduce Trace2Skill, a framework that mirrors how human experts author skills: by holistically analyzing broad execution experience before distilling it into a single, comprehensive guide. Instead of reacting sequentially to individual trajectories, Trace2Skill dispatches a parallel fleet of sub-agents to analyze a diverse pool of executions. It extracts trajectory-specific lessons and hierarchically consolidates them into a unified, conflict-free skill directory via inductive reasoning. Trace2Skill supports both deepening existing human-written skills and creating new ones from scratch. Experiments in challenging domains, such as spreadsheet, VisionQA and math reasoning, show that Trace2Skill significantly improves upon strong baselines, including Anthropic's official xlsx skills. Crucially, this trajectory-grounded evolution does not merely memorize task instances or model-specific quirks: evolved skills transfer across LLM scales and generalize to OOD settings. For example, skills evolved by Qwen3.5-35B on its own trajectories improved a Qwen3.5-122B agent by up to 57.65 absolute percentage points on WikiTableQuestions. Ultimately, our results demonstrate that complex agent experience can be packaged into highly transferable, declarative skills -- requiring no parameter updates, no external retrieval modules, and utilizing open-source models as small as 35B parameters.
2603.25158.paper_summary_zh: 為大型語言模型（LLM）代理配備特定領域的技能對於應對複雜任務至關重要。<lb/>然而，手動編寫造成了嚴重的可擴展性瓶頸。<lb/>相反，自動化技能生成往往產生脆弱或片段化的結果，因為它要麼依賴於淺層的參數知識，要麼順序過度擬合於不可泛化的軌跡本地教訓。<lb/>為了克服這一點，我們介紹了Trace2Skill，這是一個模仿人類專家編寫技能的框架：通過全面分析廣泛的執行經驗，然後將其提煉成一個單一的綜合指南。<lb/>Trace2Skill不是對單個軌跡進行順序反應，而是派遣一支平行的子代理艦隊來分析多樣化的執行池。<lb/>它提取特定於軌跡的教訓，並通過歸納推理將其層次化整合成一個統一的、無衝突的技能目錄。<lb/>Trace2Skill支持深化現有的人類編寫技能，並從零開始創建新的技能。<lb/>在挑戰性領域的實驗中，例如電子表格、VisionQA和數學推理，顯示Trace2Skill顯著超越強基準，包括Anthropic的官方xlsx技能。<lb/>至關重要的是，這種基於軌跡的演變不僅僅是記憶任務實例或模型特定的特性：演變的技能可以在LLM規模之間轉移，並且對OOD設置具有泛化能力。<lb/>例如，Qwen3.5-35B在其自身軌跡上演變的技能使得Qwen3.5-122B代理在WikiTableQuestions上提高了多達57.65個絕對百分點。<lb/>最終，我們的結果表明，複雜的代理經驗可以被打包成高度可轉移的、聲明式的技能——不需要參數更新，不需要外部檢索模塊，並利用小至35B參數的開源模型。
2603.25158.author: Jingwei Ni et.al.
2603.25158.authors: Jingwei Ni, Yihao Liu, Xinpeng Liu, Yutao Sun, Mengyu Zhou, Pengyu Cheng, Dexin Wang, Xiaoxi Jiang, Guanjun Jiang
